ELR Name: HULL STATION - SELBY (1ST PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text:
These two bridges have had continues sightings of disused Hypodermic needles located around the bridge due to a minority of carless members of pubic who are continually throwing them over the bridge sides.~
I was there this weekend carrying out inspections on the night and with the light restriction in the area it did make it hard to see the needles on track but luckily they had been highlighted with spray marks to make it more clear for other on track staff to notice the hazard. ~
As this is a continued hazard and we are unable to stop the cause at the source, I thick adding this to the hazards directory will allow track staff who have no perverse site knowledge of the area, the hazard directory will give them knowledge of the hazard.

These two bridges have had continues sightings of disused Hypodermic needles located around the bridge due to a minority of carless members of pubic who are continually throwing them over the bridge sides.~
I was there this weekend carrying out inspections on the night and with the light restriction in the area it did make it hard to see the needles on track but luckily they had been highlighted with spray marks to make it more clear for other on track staff to notice the hazard. ~
As this is a continued hazard and we are unable to stop the cause at the source, I thick adding this to the hazards directory will allow track staff who have no perverse site knowledge of the area, the hazard directory will give them knowledge of the hazard.

Free Text: As part of the MS3 Communications scheme in Hull, two parallel 125mm outside diameter PE ducts in 2 no. adjacent crossings have been installed beneath the railway using directional drilling techniques.  The directional drilling works were undertaken by IDrill on behalf of Welch Civils Ltd in order to facilitate installation of fibre cables for commercial and private broadband usage.  The finished invert level of the undertrack crossing ducts is approximately 6.0m below existing rail levels with the horizontal spacing between the two ducts being 3m. (Centreline Mileage 0m 1672yds on HUL1).  The ducts are installed at approximately 80 degrees to the line of the tracks.  Track: All/Multiple Tracks = Up and Dn Main Leeds.

Nesting Peregrine Falcons <schedule 1 protected bird> present on crimple viaduct. It is recommended that a buffer zone of 100m from the perch would be appropriate, where no works should take place. This would include the north end of the viaduct. At the south end of the viaduct track works may be undertaken, between 100m -400m from the perch, but only under the watching brief of a suitably-qualified ecologist <SQE>, who would ascertain if the works are likely to cause disturbance at any stage. Works would be terminated if this was deemed necessary by the SQE. Foot access to the site should also only be made from the south end of the viaduct. Works outside the 400m zone do not require the presence of a SQE. No intrusive works to take place which emit noise/ disturbance of the ballast during the nesting season <March - August>. For further enquiries/questions please contact Route Environment Specialist or LNE North Workforce Health, Safety & Environment Advisor.
